acceptance_targets: [chapter_03_award_001] Pass only if a cold reader can name a new tactical state by the end of the chapter that is more specific than 'Carmen is suspicious and Harry is worried' while still retaining the must-land beats of Harry walking, the ring mechanics, the coffee/compression-sock domestic comedy, Burt's Blue Note story, and Carmen as a sharper threat. Fail if the final passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:275 only sharpens or summarizes the table conversation without changing the board.

[chapter_03_craft_001] Pass only if a cold reader can answer three questions from the revised chapter alone: where the controlled stock currently comes from, why the counts can go short without immediate staff detection, and what ethical line Agnes enforces. Fail if the chapter still offers only counts and stash description without source-and-cost clarity.

[F08_ch03_missing_phone_seed] Read lines 460-490. (1) At least one moment exists that, on rereading after chapter 17, would make a reader think 'that was the second phone' or 'that explains the second phone.' (2) The seed does not call attention to itself on first read — it passes as character texture. (3) Harry's established habits (pencil notebook, filing, contingency thinking) are not disrupted by the addition.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if a cold reader can name a new tactical state by the end of the chapter that is more specific than 'Carmen is suspicious and Harry is worried' while still retaining the must-land beats of Harry walking, the ring mechanics, the coffee/compression-sock domestic comedy, Burt's Blue Note story, and Carmen as a sharper threat. Fail if the final passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:275 only sharpens or summarizes the table conversation without changing the board.
findings.problem: The chapter lands as a highly controlled reveal scene rather than a chapter-defining turn. Once the cash split, black-notebook risk, and Carmen discussion are legible, the closing pages mostly restate the same danger in cleaner language instead of forcing a new tactical state, so the chapter reads as setup carried by excellent narration rather than shortlist-level consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the core reveal beats, but repurpose the long explanatory material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:97 and the closing bed passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:275 so the Carmen discussion beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:171 produces one concrete operational change, miscalculation, or explicit choice whose consequence is already in motion by the last line.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if a cold reader can answer three questions from the revised chapter alone: where the controlled stock currently comes from, why the counts can go short without immediate staff detection, and what ethical line Agnes enforces. Fail if the chapter still offers only counts and stash description without source-and-cost clarity.
findings.problem: For a chapter whose main job is revealing the ring, the extraction logic behind the controlled stock stays blurrier than it should. The reader gets stash size, inventory counts, buyer-code records, and Lola's theatrical swallow, but not a clean on-page answer to which controlled meds are genuine surplus, which are diverted from currently prescribed residents, what resident-level cost makes that possible, or where Agnes's safety line sits. That weakens both moral ambiguity and local cause-and-effect.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:61 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:67, or in the Carmen-risk exchange beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:185, add one or two specific beats that categorize the stock: whose prescriptions feed the oxycodone/alprazolam/tramadol, how the diversion is physically managed at med pass, and what Agnes will not pull or sell. If some inventory comes at real resident cost, state that cost plainly instead of leaving it as soft inference.

(2) The seed does not call attention to itself on first read — it passes as character texture. (3) Harry's established habits (pencil notebook, filing, contingency thinking) are not disrupted by the addition.

evidence.excerpt: 465: Harry noted it in his own notebook — pocket-sized, pencil, because pencil could be erased and Harry had learned in forty years of accounting that permanence was a liability. That was four hundred off the month before. The gap bothered him, even though he knew the reason.

477: Harry watched him for another second, then let it go. Earl's dismissals were non-negotiable, and pushing past them wasn't a move that worked with a man who'd spent his career deciding what deserved a response and what didn't. Harry filed the cough. He filed everything.

489: Harry let the silence end the exchange. Agnes didn't need prompting and she didn't tolerate it. He turned to the cash on the table and continued recording the distribution in his notebook — small, careful pencil marks, the numbers squared and legible, each entry dated, each amount verified against Earl's count. He'd done this kind of work for forty years in offices with fluorescent lighting not so different from this, except the offices had been in Bergen County and the clients had been small businesses with tax problems and the only criminal liability had been the occasional undeclared cash payment, which was a misdemeanor, not a federal charge carrying a mandatory minimum.

findings.problem: This chapter establishes Harry's operational habits in detail — his pencil notebook (line 465), his accounting mindset, his habit of filing everything (line 477), his meticulous cash-distribution tracking (line 489). But it plants no seed for a second communication device. The prepaid phone is introduced in chapter 6 as the ring's shared burner. Harry's redundancy-thinking and contingency-planning are established, but nothing in this chapter hints that Harry might maintain a private channel the ring doesn't know about. When the second phone appears in chapter 17, there is no prior physical detail to anchor it. This is a missing-setup companion to finding F07.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:465-489 (the section where Harry's habits are established during the ring meeting): plant a light seed for a second device. Strategy option A: during the cash-distribution scene, Harry reaches into his cardigan pocket, touches something, and puts it back — a gesture small enough to read as habitual but specific enough that a rereader would recognize it as the second phone. Strategy option B: in Harry's interior narration about contingency and redundancy (around line 477, 'Harry filed the cough. He filed everything'), add a thought about backup systems — 'He kept his own records because he didn't trust anyone else's numbers, and he kept his own lines of communication for the same reason.' Strategy option C: during the discussion of the prepaid phone, have Harry note to himself that one phone is a single point of failure. The seed should be no more than 1-2 sentences and should not draw attention to itself on first read.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
